name: Inventions
description: Inventors, groundbreaking devices, and technological firsts

questions.q: Who is credited with inventing the computer mouse in the 1960s?

source.notes: Demonstrated 1968 in the 'Mother of All Demos.' Shared credit with Bill English.

questions.q: The transistor, foundational to modern electronics, was invented in what year?

source.notes: At Bell Labs by Bardeen, Brattain, and Shockley. They won the 1956 Nobel Prize.

questions.q: What did Edison famously utter as the first audio recording on his phonograph?
choices: Four score and seven years ago

source.notes: Recorded December 1877. Original recording lost but Edison re-recorded it later.

questions.q: Who invented the lie detector (polygraph) in 1921?

source.notes: Berkeley police officer and medical student. Later had reservations about his own invention's accuracy.

questions.q: Who invented the zipper as we know it today?

source.notes: Patented the 'clasp locker' in 1893. Gideon Sundback refined it to the modern version in 1913.

questions.q: Who invented the World Wide Web at CERN in 1989?

source.notes: British computer scientist. Later knighted. Gave away the technology freely.

questions.q: Who invented the steamboat that traveled on the Hudson River in 1807?

source.notes: His 'Clermont' made the NYC-Albany trip in 32 hours.

questions.q: Who's credited with inventing the telephone in 1876?

source.notes: Beat Elisha Gray to the patent office by hours. Scottish-American.

questions.q: Who invented the kaleidoscope in 1816?

source.notes: Scottish physicist. Also known for Brewster's angle in optics.

questions.q: Who invented the modern analytical geometry (using x and y coordinates)?

source.notes: Published 1637 in 'La Géométrie.' Cartesian coordinates named after him.

questions.q: Who invented the first practical airplane and made the first powered flight in 1903?

source.notes: Orville and Wilbur Wright. First flight at Kitty Hawk, NC, December 17, 1903.

questions.q: Which Swedish inventor created dynamite in 1867?

source.notes: Founded the Nobel Prizes in his will to offset his legacy as 'the merchant of death.'

questions.q: Which invention by Charles Fey in 1895 pioneered slot machine gaming?

source.notes: Liberty Bell — first successful three-reel slot machine. Mechanic in San Francisco.

questions.q: Who invented the practical incandescent light bulb?

source.notes: Edison (USA) and Swan (UK) developed independently around the same time (1879). They later merged companies.

questions.q: Who is credited with inventing the first radio/wireless telegraphy?

source.notes: Italian inventor. Tesla's work predated, but Marconi got the patent and Nobel Prize (1909).

questions.q: Who invented the first practical steam engine in 1712?

source.notes: Newcomen's atmospheric engine. James Watt later improved it significantly in the 1760s-70s.

questions.q: Who invented Braille writing for the blind?

source.notes: Invented at age 15, published 1829. Braille himself was blinded in childhood.

questions.q: Which invention by Igor Sikorsky revolutionized flight in the 1930s?

source.notes: Russian-American engineer. VS-300 prototype flew 1939. First mass-produced helicopter.

questions.q: Which fundamental optical device did Galileo improve for astronomy in 1609?

source.notes: Dutch lens-makers invented the telescope ~1608. Galileo improved it and pointed it at the sky.

questions.q: Which technology did Philo Farnsworth invent, first transmitted in 1927?

source.notes: First successful electronic television. Developed his design as a teenager.
